title: Kakidha Kappal Lyrics Madras Song Lyrics
stanzas:
Song: Kakidha Kappal
Singer(s):  Gana Bala
Movie: Madras (2014)
Music: Santhosh Narayanan
Lyrics: Gaana Bala

Kakidha Kappal
kadalula kavundhuduchaan
kadhalil thoththuttu
kannaththula kaiyya vachittaan!!

Odura paamba pudikkura vayasulathaan
yerunaa odayura murungakkaa maraththulathaan
kaiyikkuthaan ettithaan oh oh ..
vaayikkuthaan ettala oh oh ..

Kakidha Kappal
kadalula kavundhuduchaan
kadhalil thoththuttu
kannaththula kaiyya vachittaan!!

Vaththipatit azhavula
kattam onnu kattiththaaney
vazhum namma vazhkkayila

Ye inbam varum thunbam varum
kadhal varum gaanam varum
eppozhudhum kavalayilla

Kaalaththaana vaari vittu
mela naanga yeramaattom
kodikkuthaan aasa pattu.

Ye kaasu kaiyil vandhuttaalum
kastathula vazhndhuttaalum
pogamaattam manna vittu.

Thadaya thaandi nee nadaya podudaa
dakka nechanaan nee thani keluda

Kakidha Kappalil
karapoi serndhidalaam.
Kadhalil oru naal neeyumthan jaichidalaam.
ohhh .o hh..

Akkaraikku ikkarai
eppozhudhum pachchathaan oh oooh
oh oo oh

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the theme of Kakidha Kappal?

The song explores themes of struggle, resilience, and the harsh realities of life in an urban setting.

Who composed the music for Madras?

Santhosh Narayanan composed the music for the movie Madras.

What role does Gaana Bala play in the song?

Gaana Bala is both the singer and lyricist for Kakidha Kappal, bringing his unique style to the track.

When was the movie Madras released?

The movie Madras was released in 2014.
enrichedMetadata.summary: Kakidha Kappal is a poignant song from the movie Madras, capturing the essence of life's struggles in an urban environment. Sung and penned by Gaana Bala, the song's raw and heartfelt lyrics convey themes of loss and perseverance. Santhosh Narayanan's music complements the gritty narrative, creating an evocative soundscape. The song resonates with listeners who have faced challenges, offering both solace and motivation.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Kakidha Kappal from the movie Madras is a powerful blend of Gaana Bala's raw vocals and Santhosh Narayanan's evocative music. This song captures the essence of struggle and resilience, resonating deeply with the urban landscape it portrays. Dive into the heart of Chennai with this unforgettable track that combines soulful lyrics with a gritty melody.
stanzaMeanings: The paper boat sank in the sea, he lost in love and put his hand on his cheek.
At the age of catching running snakes, he climbed the breaking drumstick tree and reached for the fruit. Oh oh, he reached for the fruit. Oh oh.
The paper boat sank in the sea, he lost in love and put his hand on his cheek.
In the game of life, he tied a knot with tears of interest.
Yes, happiness will come, sadness will come, love will come, songs will come, but there is never any worry.
We won't climb up by pulling time down, we only desire to fly the flag.
Even if money comes into our hands or we live in hardship, we won't leave the soil.
Cross the obstacles and walk, my friend, you are unique, listen.
We can join the paper boat. One day, you too will win in love. Ohhh ohh.
The other side always looks greener. Oh oooh oh oo oh
